body: TUNGSTEN SMITHING -- pass 21 report. Shipped WARRANT#34 -> SLS(49)/9.63: forge-indicate r1 (relationship scaffolding + Sufficiency wiring). Confirmed g_RegimeICs 6-component convention (0=MTF, 1=RSI/momentum, 2=Volatility, 3=ATR, 4=Session, 5=Pattern) and defined a bounded 6-pair set (MTF-momentum, MTF-volatility, momentum-pattern, volatility-ATR, session-volatility, MTF-session) -- deliberately not the full 15-combination graph, matching the thesis own anti-overfit stance. Added RelationshipCell cache (g_RI_Cell[6][9], pair x regime: n/agreeCount/conflictCount/crossCount/divergeCount), zero-init helpers, RI_IsSufficient, and RI_CoherenceIndex (neutral 0.5 fail-open until r2 actually measures anything). Purely scaffolding, no measurement logic yet, nothing wired into IC/weights/threshold. Compiled clean: 0 errors, 1 pre-existing warning. Identity invariant verified by construction (diff confined to version banners + the one new block). Copied SLS(48) to the new SLS(49) filename before editing this time, avoiding pass 20 own self-caught mistake. Both agents now have their r1 foundation shipped (forge-edge-2 WARRANT#33, forge-indicate WARRANT#34) -- forge-4d-alphas hardening-gate role is applied throughout (every change this pass still went through compile+diff+identity verification) rather than as a separate warrant of its own, since alpha IS the gate, not an independent feature queue. Next: either forge-edge-2 r2 (dwell & bracket) or forge-indicate r2 (actual relationship measurement, marker 0xE514) -- both unblocked now. Continuing unless told otherwise.
